## Implementation

The model functions live in `src/arty/fragmentation.py` — `gurney_velocity`,
`mott_params`, `mott_N`, `retardation_coeff`, `ke_at_range`,
`min_lethal_mass`, `pk_given_hit`, `expected_kills`, and `p_kill`. The
notebook imports and calls them; no physics is reimplemented here.

```{python}
V0 = gurney_velocity(shell)
mu, N0 = mott_params(shell, V0)

print("=== Gurney ===")
print(f"  V0               : {V0:.0f} m/s")

print("\n=== Mott ===")
print(f"  Half-weight μ    : {mu*1e3:.2f} g")
print(f"  Avg frag mass 2μ : {2*mu*1e3:.2f} g")
print(f"  Mode mass  μ/4   : {mu/4*1e3:.2f} g")
print(f"  Total frags N0   : {N0:.0f}")

# Cavity/wall-thickness ratio: doctrinal break-up diagnostic.
# r_inner and wall_t are the initial (pre-detonation) shell geometry from §3.
cavity_wall_ratio = (2 * r_inner) / wall_t   # inner diameter / wall thickness
print(f"  Cavity/wall-thickness ratio (d_i / t_w) : {cavity_wall_ratio:.1f}")
print(f"    Doctrinal reference: 25-pdr ≈ 4, ideal ≈ 10")
print(f"    Higher ratio → more, smaller fragments (lower μ)")
for m_g in [0.5, 1, 5, 10, 50]:
    print(f"  N(m > {m_g:4.1f} g)   : {mott_N(np.array([m_g*1e-3]), N0, mu)[0]:.0f}")
```

______________________________________________________________________
